items.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

items.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue documents gaps in the README files for `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` and `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` following PR #58274, which added `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`, deterministic `agent_session_id` derivation, and handler-level structured logging but did not update the corresponding documentation sections.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` (1.0.0-beta.22), `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` (1.0.0-beta.2)
**Affected API:** `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`, `agent_session_id` derivation, handler-level structured logging, `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`
**Scenarios:**
- Core README Key Concepts and Troubleshooting/Logging sections are missing documentation of `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` and its automatic registration
- Responses README Key Concepts section is missing documentation of deterministic `agent_session_id` derivation logic and fallback behavior
- Responses README Troubleshooting/Logging section does not describe handler-level structured logs or `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`

**Root ask:** Update both READMEs to document newly added logging features and session ID derivation so developers can discover and configure these behaviors.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only gap (no runtime bug). The implementation guide in the issue provides exact Markdown edits for both README files including tables, configuration examples, and fallback priority lists.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review PR #58274 (commit 48e9f7bd) to confirm the actual behavior of `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` and `agent_session_id` derivation matches the documentation proposed in the issue body
2. Apply the edits described in the Implementation Guide to `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` and `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md`
3. Verify no existing tests or snippets reference the old Logging section text; run `dotnet build` and `dotnet test --filter TestCategory!=Live` for both packages to confirm no regressions
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — These are data-plane ASP.NET Core server-side SDK packages (`Azure.AI.AgentServer.*`), not management plane or provisioning libraries
- **Service:** `AI Agents` — The packages reside in `sdk/agentserver/`, which maps to the `%AI Agents` ServiceLabel in CODEOWNERS (line 85)
- **Confidence:** High — The service directory `sdk/agentserver/` is unambiguous, the category is clearly data-plane client, and the CODEOWNERS mapping is direct
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %AI Agents` (line 85) — the issue concerns `sdk/agentserver/` packages which map directly to the `%AI Agents` label
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** `@ankitbko` `@RaviPidaparthi`
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; `@mentioned` `@ankitbko` and `@RaviPidaparthi`; issue left unassigned (ServiceOwners path, no AzureSdkOwners)
- **Scan notes:** No management-specific or other multi-label entries were relevant; the `%AI Agents` entry at line 85 was the only candidate and matched immediately
</details>
